trivia: The definitive short-haul jet of the 1960s and 70s: T-tail, rear engines, and self-sufficient airstairs for small airports. Its DNA ran unbroken through the MD-80, MD-90 and 717.
Practically extinct in service, which is exactly why the identity is preserved: catches deserve names even when history has moved on.
funFacts: The most numerous DC-9 type, with over 600 built.
Founded a fuselage lineage that lasted 40 years.
